combat_history.event: Tuo Jiang conducted live-fire testing of Hsiung Feng III missiles, successfully engaging surface targets at maximum range
combat_history.significance: First operational validation of supersonic anti-ship missile capability from the platform

combat_history.event: Multiple Tuo Jiang-class vessels conducted patrol operations during heightened tensions following Pelosi visit, maintaining readiness posture
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated rapid deployment capability and sustained operations during crisis period

modernization.description: Integration of indigenous TC-2N surface-to-air missiles to provide point air defense capability against aircraft and incoming missiles
modernization.impact: Would address critical air defense vulnerability, enhancing survivability in contested airspace

modernization.description: Upgraded electronic warfare systems including improved ESM/ECM capabilities and decoy systems
modernization.impact: Better survivability against modern radar-guided threats and improved situational awareness

modernization.description: Potential integration of land-attack cruise missile variant for coastal defense and counter-landing operations
modernization.impact: Would provide precision strike capability against landing forces and coastal installations

vulnerabilities.description: Limited air defense beyond single Phalanx CIWS, highly vulnerable to air attack from fighter aircraft or helicopters
vulnerabilities.context: In Taiwan Strait scenario, PLAAF would likely achieve local air superiority, making survival dependent on stealth and speed
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Planned TC-2N SAM integration, emphasis on rapid hit-and-run tactics

vulnerabilities.description: Short range and limited sea-keeping ability restricts operations to coastal waters, cannot sustain extended patrols
vulnerabilities.context: Design trade-off for speed and stealth limits strategic flexibility and operational tempo
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Distributed basing strategy and emphasis on surge operations rather than continuous patrol

vulnerabilities.description: Single radar system creates vulnerability to jamming or failure, limited over-the-horizon targeting capability
vulnerabilities.context: Dependent on external targeting for maximum-range Hsiung Feng III engagements against maneuvering targets
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Integration with Taiwan's coastal radar network and potential datalink improvements

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Coastal patrol and rapid response from distributed bases, surge deployment during crisis
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Often operates individually or in pairs, occasionally with larger ROCN task forces
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High maintenance requirements for waterjet propulsion, limited by crew training pipeline

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Chinese counterpart with similar catamaran hull but smaller, less sophisticated sensors and older anti-ship missiles. Tuo Jiang has better sea-keeping and longer-range weapons.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: How Taiwan's corvettes plan to swarm Chinese fast attack craft

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Israeli design emphasizes air defense with Barak missiles and multi-mission capability. Tuo Jiang sacrifices versatility for pure anti-ship firepower and speed.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Different approaches to small nation naval strategy: Air defense vs anti-ship focus

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Korean PKX-B design balances anti-ship and anti-submarine warfare with more conservative conventional hull. Tuo Jiang prioritizes speed and stealth over ASW capability.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Asian corvette design philosophy: Balanced capability vs specialized threat focus

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Japanese design influenced Tuo Jiang development but focuses on Type 90 missiles with shorter range. Tuo Jiang has superior stealth characteristics and longer-range weapons.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Evolution of Pacific fast attack craft: From Japanese influence to Taiwanese innovation

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Norwegian air-cushion catamaran inspired Taiwan's stealth approach but designed for Arctic conditions. Tuo Jiang adapted concept for tropical littoral warfare with heavier armament.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Stealth corvette concepts: From Norwegian fjords to Taiwan Strait adaptation
